retrieved_note: Pilot agent extraction from official public pages and SOC PDF. Normalize before shipping to UI.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Shared public eligibility grid across Classic, Gold, and Platinum.

The lowest published salaried threshold on the official page is PKR 35,000 for GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent employees; approved-company and non-approved-company applicants are shown at higher salary bands.
SEB/SEP applicants are shown separately on the official page with PKR 150,000 average-balance criterion.
The FAQ on the same page splits maximum age as 60 for permanent salaried, 65 for contractual salaried/pensioners, and 70 for SEB/SEP.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Eligibility comes from the shared Mastercard page rather than a Gold-only page.
The lowest published salaried threshold on that page is PKR 35,000 for GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent employees; other salaried categories are higher.
SEB/SEP applicants are shown separately with PKR 150,000 average-balance criterion.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Eligibility comes from the shared Mastercard page rather than a Platinum-only page.
The lowest published salaried threshold on that page is PKR 35,000 for GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent employees; other salaried categories are higher.
SEB/SEP applicants are shown separately with PKR 150,000 average-balance criterion.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Product page directly exposes the higher World eligibility thresholds.
The official World page publishes minimum income as PKR 1.00 million for salaried applicants and PKR 2.00 million for SEB/SEP applicants.
No separate public deposit-relationship threshold was found on the World card page in this pass.
Card page says no minimum balance requirement for issuance or retention.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Relationship-based eligibility page shows the card for customers maintaining PKR 5,000,000 quarterly average relationship; SOC lists PKR 18,000 standard annual fee.

notes: Eligibility is relationship-balance based rather than salary based on the official card page.
The page shows PKR 5,000,000 quarterly average relationship for conventional current, Islamic current, Islamic savings, or CASA relationship.
PayPak debit issuance is account-linked rather than salary-gated on the public card page.
Official Askari Asaan Current and Asaan Saving account pages surface PKR 100 opening amount and no minimum balance requirement.
Those no-minimum-balance account pages advertise PayPak debit card issuance, while the SOC provides the standard joining and annual fee.

gaps: Debit-card pages do not clearly publish age criteria.
Debit cards are mostly account-linked, so salary requirements are usually absent.
Some shared credit-card eligibility values need later refinement by applicant category rather than one single threshold.
